based_on: Byte-precise mutation of fixtures/mazur.golden.jsonl (engine-emitted canonical bytes). The fixture differs from the golden in exactly two places: the fixture_status block (3 fields) and the single targeted numeric value.

what_was_NOT_mutated: updates[4].gradient (-0.0821670406 — Rule 4 still passes: product(factors) still equals stored gradient)
updates[4].weight_after (0.358916480 — derived from the ORIGINAL update; Rule 6 will cascade since stored weight_after no longer equals weight_before + mutated update)
parameters_after (consistent with the ORIGINAL update; Rule 7 will cascade if it uses the mutated update value)

single_target_invariant: Reconciliation produces exactly one Rule 5 failure naming updates[4].update on parameter w5. Rule 4 must NOT fire (gradient is unchanged). Rules 6 and 7 cascade from Rule 5 — both observe parameter_id 'w5' with cascade_of_rule = 5.
cascading_failures_observed: Rule 6 on updates[4].weight_after: stored weight_after (0.358916480) was derived from original update; recomputed = weight_before + mutated update differs by ~1e-6 (cascade_of_rule: 5)
Rule 7 on parameters_after.w5: stored parameters_after.w5 (0.358916480) was derived from original update; recomputed = parameters_before.w5 + mutated update differs by ~1e-6 (cascade_of_rule: 5)
failure_priority_assertion: Per docs/reconciliation.md, reconciliation failures report before fixture-lifecycle failures.
purpose: Anti-circularity (Csmith / CompCert lineage): proves the reconciler checks Rule 5 (update == lr × gradient) by detecting a 1e-6 mismatch (1000x the 1e-9 tolerance) between stored update and the product of (unmutated) lr × gradient. Cascade into Rules 6, 7 demonstrates the cascade_of_rule wiring.
